title: equisetum palustre
intro: Equisetum palustre, also known as marsh horsetail, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the genus Equisetum, a group of vascular plants that are thought to be one of the most ancient living plant families. The medicinal properties of Equisetum palustre include its high silica content, which can help to improve skin and hair health, as well as its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in wound healing and the treatment of urinary tract infections. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to improve digestion, reduce stress and anxiety, and boost the immune system. Equisetum palustre is typically prepared as a tea, tincture, or powder for herbal use, with the root being used more frequently than the aerial parts for medicinal purposes.
